text: Standing outside the glass-walled room, I feel an odd distance from the world of ordered collaboration and presentation that it represents. It is clean, bright, efficient—a world designed for clarity and shared purpose. But upon stepping into the exhibition space and facing the richly colored wall and evocative words, my mood shifts. The invitation to reflect on uncertainty and obscurity resonates deeply; the world here is not about answers, but about the breadth of inquiry and the bounds of human imagination. Here, I feel both unsettled and inspired, aware of how artificial clarity can be and how much there is to wonder about in the shadows.
